example_ai_output.program_summary: Week 3 of HWPO accumulation phase. Focus on building squat strength and improving snatch technique. Volume remains high with 5 training days. Metabolic conditioning targets lactate threshold.

monday.name: Heavy Squat + Short AMRAP
monday.description: Back squat volume day with high-rep accessory work. Short, intense metcon to develop lactate tolerance without excessive fatigue.
monday.warm_up: 15min: Row 500m easy, then 2 rounds (10 air squats, 10 PVC pass-throughs, 5 inchworms, 5 spiderman lunges per side). Squat-specific: 10 goblet squats, 10 cossack squats, wall sits 30s x 2

movements.notes: Focus on hitting depth (hip crease below knee), maintain upright torso, drive knees out. Tempo: controlled down (3s), explosive up (1s). Film last set for form check.
Rear foot elevated on bench. Front shin vertical, drive through heel. Control 2s descent, explode up. Address quad/glute imbalances.
Hamstring isolation after heavy squats. 2s squeeze at top, slow eccentric. Superset with next if short on time.
Barbell (43kg bar). Aim for unbroken sets. Full lockout overhead, full depth squat. Pacing: quick but sustainable.
Full chest-to-ground burpee, jump to box, step down. No rebounds. Control landing.
Damper 10, hold 2:00/500m pace or faster. Strong legs, patient hands. Keep pulling!

parts.pacing_strategy: First 3 rounds: smooth, unbroken thrusters. Rounds 4+: break thrusters 3-2 if needed. Consistent row pace throughout.
parts.target_stimulus: Lactate threshold, mental toughness. Should feel spicy but not soul-crushing.

monday.cooldown: 10min: Easy bike 5min (Zone 1 HR), then foam roll quads 2min, hamstrings 2min, glutes 1min. Couch stretch 2min per side.
monday.target_stimulus: Squat strength development + high-rep barbell cycling under fatigue

scaling_options.beginner: Goblet squat 5x8 @ 24kg KB, Goblet thrusters, Box step-ups, Bike 20 cal instead of row

tuesday.name: Olympic Lifting + Gymnastics
tuesday.description: Technical snatch work with volume. Muscle-up progression. Moderate metcon to maintain conditioning without interfering with skill work.
tuesday.warm_up: 15min: 400m jog, then 3 rounds (5 snatch grip deadlifts with empty bar, 10 scap pull-ups, 5 slow wall walks, 10 PVC overhead squats). Mobility: shoulder dislocates 20x, wrist prep, ankle rocks.

movements.notes: Complex = Hang Power Snatch (above knee) + Power Snatch (from floor) + Overhead Squat. Focus: fast elbows, catch high and ride down, active shoulders in OHS. Week 3 = volume work, not max effort. Quality over load.

parts.coaching_cues: Setup: wide grip (fingertips to opposite shoulder). Pull: vertical bar path, keep it close. Turnover: aggressive elbows, punch under fast. Catch: heels down, weight in midfoot. This is skill work - miss a rep? Lower weight.

movements.notes: Strict deficit if possible (start from bottom of dip). If not proficient: Russian dips 6x5 or ring transitions 6x3. Goal: build strength for MU.
Freestanding against wall. Hollow body, tight core, active shoulders. Progress to handstand walk if holds are solid 60s+.
Light weight, fast cycling. Touch-and-go reps. Don't drop bar.
Kipping allowed. Chest makes contact with bar at sternum height. Scale: regular pull-ups.
Nose/forehead to ground, full lockout. Kipping allowed. Scale: Push-ups to parallette or abmat under head.

parts.pacing_strategy: Round of 21: Break early (11-10 or 7-7-7). Round of 15: minimize breaks. Round of 9: push hard, small sets ok.
parts.target_stimulus: Moderate intensity, gymnastic stamina. Should finish tired but not destroyed.

tuesday.cooldown: 12min: Row 1000m easy pace, then shoulder stretches (doorway pec stretch 90s per side, cross-body stretch, lat stretch on rig). Wrist stretches.

scaling_options.scaled: Snatch 25kg, Pull-ups (not C2B), HSPU with 1 abmat
scaling_options.beginner: Hang power snatch 15kg, Ring rows, Push-ups

wednesday.name: Active Recovery + Midline
wednesday.description: Low-intensity aerobic work to promote recovery. Core strength focus. Stay in Zone 2 heart rate (conversational pace).
wednesday.warm_up: 10min: Easy bike 5min, then 2 rounds (10 cat-cows, 10 bird-dogs per side, plank 30s, side plank 20s per side)

movements.notes: Conversational pace, nose breathing if possible. HR should stay in Zone 2 (130-150 bpm for most).
Smooth, relaxed. No sprinting. Should be able to hold conversation.
Damper 5-6, long strokes, patient hands. Recovery row.

parts.target_stimulus: Aerobic base building, flush out yesterday's legs. NOT a metcon. Stay easy.
parts.notes: Remaining time each 5min = rest. This should feel restorative, not challenging.

movements.notes: Full range, control on way up. If new to GHD, start with 10-12 reps.
GHD or back extension bench. Slow tempo, squeeze glutes at top.
Low back pressed to floor, arms overhead, legs 6 inches off ground. Break into sets if needed.
Forearm plank, straight line from head to heels. Squeeze glutes, brace core.

parts.notes: Rest 60-90s between rounds. Focus on quality contractions, not speed.

scaling_options.All levels: Keep intensity LOW. If you're breathing hard, slow down.

thursday.name: Heavy Deadlift + Interval Running
thursday.description: Posterior chain strength. Running intervals for conditioning. Complements Monday's squat day.
thursday.warm_up: 15min: Row 800m easy, then 3 rounds (10 good mornings with PVC, 10 glute bridges, 5 inchworms). Deadlift-specific: 5 reps @ empty bar, 5 @ 40%, 3 @ 60%, 1 @ 70%

movements.notes: Work up to heavy single, NOT max. Should feel hard but leave 1 rep in tank. Focus: tight setup, drive through floor, finish with glutes.
Drop weight, work on speed. Explosive off floor, control lockout.
Hamstring stretch, hinge at hips. Bar stays close to legs. Feel it in hamstrings.
400m repeats. Goal: consistent splits across all 6. Target 1:30-1:45 per lap. Walk/jog during rest.

parts.target_stimulus: Anaerobic threshold, leg speed after heavy deads. Legs will be fatigued - that's the point.
parts.pacing_strategy: First 2 rounds: dial in pace. Rounds 3-6: hold or negative split. Don't go out too hot.

thursday.cooldown: 10min: Walk 400m, foam roll posterior chain (glutes, hamstrings, calves), pigeon stretch 2min per side

friday.name: Competition WOD Simulation
friday.description: Long, mixed-modal workout simulating competition environment. Full effort. Week 3 benchmark.
friday.warm_up: 20min: General warm-up (row 1000m, 3 rounds of 10 air squats, 10 push-ups, 10 sit-ups), then specific movement prep for workout (light rounds of each movement)

movements.notes: Touch and go or singles. Full lockout each rep.
Step down. No rebounds. Full hip extension at top.
American swings (overhead). Russian (eye level) is scaled.
20lb ball to 10ft target. Full squat depth, hit target.
All out. Don't pace this. Just row.
Chest to ground, jump over imaginary bar. Or lateral burpees over rower.

parts.pacing_strategy: This is a grinder. Pace early, push late. Break deads into manageable sets (10-10-10 or 15-15). Box jumps: steady rhythm. KB swings: big sets early. Wall balls: don't stop moving. Row: give everything left. Burpees: stay moving, small steps ok.
parts.target_stimulus: Mental toughness, work capacity, suffer fest. This should hurt. Log time as benchmark.

friday.cooldown: 15min: Walk 800m slowly, static stretching (everything), ice bath if available, nutrition immediately after

scaling_options.scaled: Deadlift 80kg, Box 20in, KB 24kg, Wall ball 6kg to 9ft, Row 50 cal, Burpees 60

friday.notes: Record time. This is a benchmark for week 3. You'll repeat similar workout in week 7 to measure progress.
